item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was so cool. It really took me by surprise and works great as a parody of the overly serious sarkic stuff. Well done! I wish this one was as upvoted as it deserves to be. Take mine at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoting it now, so it gets a rewrite. The concept is good, but some things are off here.

First, the entities didn't needed to be identified in blue. That seemed at first to play a role later, but did not. By the way, there is no visual cue as to the position of a Sarkicist in the religion, so identifying the Karcist and Võlutaar just by reviewing the feed seems to not be a thing. If you want, just make as you did with the Halkost and spell it out "live" in the log.

Second, the description of the area is too confusing. I have no clear idea of what the superimposed 5 meters perimeter looks like, if there is a cave or not, how you are supposed to interact with the anomaly, and even how things were during the exploration. That all need a heavy rework just to make things simpler to understand.

Third, as others pointed, it just feels unfinished. I don't think the Foundation have reason to shut down new explorations, by the way, neither does it really seems to be keter at all. You don't need to actually describe further efforts to understand and document the anomaly, but just implying that it is intended to be explored soon could be enough, if you don't want to extend it.

Last, but not least, I'm really missing more skips with visual addenda lately, and this one seems perfect for this. You get a photo of the bay and we have it. Nowadays it seems that only format screws get to have images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really know what to say, I mean for one point I like the new take on the Sarkist but, in my opinion, this could be one of the best jokes SCP or a tale rather than a regular SCP item, also in th beggining I don't see why it was necesary to describe the Karcist and everyone else when in the log it doesn't seem to pay much of a role on their nature, I even beleive it would've been a better punchline if the readers noticed at the end that all of this was from some "apocaliptic" yet stupid people in charge of a great weapon.
In the end I beleive it has potential, however there is still some work to do, hope to see some improvment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That feels a tad too short. I think you should have made a few more logs with dumb Sarkics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite liked this, it was a nice change of pace from most apocalyptically dangerous Sarkic stuff, and also shows that Sarkites are, in the end, just people: inept in all the ways the rest of us are. While the aquatic environment felt new, I feel the absurd humour was the primary good point of this, as it was quite entertaining.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I can understand that. Opinion appears to be mixed about it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't a huge fan of the log at the end, it felt like a bit of buildup for a weak payoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll see what I can do - tomorrow though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure exactly - some kind of denouement that settles things, or some closing statement aside from what's included in the exploration log. Given the tone of the article, something humorous or possibly inane (on the instances' part) should suffice. Perhaps take a look at other articles this is lambasting and see how they end - (usually with some form of 'oh shit' stinger) and twist that into something that'd elicit all the 'lol's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. What else do you feel I should add?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, it kinda peters out after the log, without a strong resolution. I'd work towards something to tie it together at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reluctant upvote due solely to the instances' bumbling being a nice change of pace to the usual Sarknanniggans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to make it so that it didn't take itself too seriously, but at the same time it still had enough cohesion for a story. Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is rather silly and a little cliche, but in a way I'm a huge fan of.
I'm a sucker for stories where the absurdity of reality collides with ordinarily serious tropes.
I lost it at "Go off to do whatever things you usually do?".

item.title: Nice.
item.content:encoded: It left me both creeped out and laughing. Take my +1, you clever (wo)man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was sitting in my sandbox for so long I was afraid it would never see the light of day.

This draft was a work in progress for about a month. I was working on it on and off, and only managed to get any sort of serious work done on it recently. A big thanks to everyone who looked at this draft over the time it was floating around in my sandbox, I don't remember all of them aside from Lazar Lystanuk and Tretter who both looked it over recently.
